touches.body: {{first_name}}, seeing {{company}} gear up for RSA Conference 2026. Your peers are often challenged with proving event pipeline and attribution to their CMOs.

touches.subject: rsa pipeline attribution
touches.body: looks like {{company}} is prepping for RSA Conference, which suggests you're probably focused on proving event ROI for that budget line. how are you ensuring your post-RSA follow-up translates into clear pipeline attribution, especially with those longer CISO sales cycles? Palo Alto Networks and CrowdStrike are using us to attribute 3x more pipeline directly to event activity compared to their old models. it involves linking post-event engagement to specific deal stages. worth a look?

noticed RSA Conference is just a week away, {{first_name}}, which often brings up challenges around proving event ROI for {{company}}. How are you ensuring your RSA booth activity translates into a clear, provable pipeline number for your board? Open to comparing notes on how others handle this?

looks like you're at Moscone for RSA this week, {{first_name}}, which suggests proving pipeline ROI from the show is a priority for {{company}}. how are you ensuring your event spend connects directly to closed-won revenue? Cloudflare and Palo Alto Networks track 85% of their RSA pipeline to deals, compared to 15% before, using our attribution mapping. open to learning more?

validation_errors.message: Body has 4 sentences; expected max 3 for linkedin_day_of_nudge.

touches.subject: rsa pipeline attribution
touches.body: noticed {{company}} exhibited at RSA Conference and it often brings up questions about proving pipeline attribution from those events, {{first_name}}. How are you ensuring event-sourced pipeline from RSA gets properly attributed, especially with the long sales cycles in cybersecurity? Palo Alto Networks and CrowdStrike use us to attribute 35% more pipeline from events compared to their previous last-touch models by mapping early-stage engagement to later-stage deal progression. Worth a look?

Looks like RSA Conference just wrapped up, {{first_name}}. For VPs of Marketing at {{company}}, how are you ensuring your RSA budget is fully reflected on the attribution slide? Cybersecurity leaders like Fortinet and Okta are using us to attribute 60% of their event pipeline to closed-won deals, compared to 15% before, by linking booth engagement to multi-touch models. Worth a look?
noticed you're prepping for RSA Conference 2026 at {{company}}, {{first_name}}. other demand leaders are streamlining their event outreach before day one.

touches.subject: event lead follow up
touches.body: noticed {{company}} is likely preparing for RSA Conference, which suggests {{first_name}}, you're probably thinking about how to get those booth scans to reps quickly. how are you ensuring event leads from RSA get to reps and convert before they go cold? Salesforce and HubSpot use us to get event leads to SDRs in 2 hours, compared to 72 hours with spreadsheets. it involves an automated sync that bypasses manual data entry. worth a look?

With RSA Conference 2026 next week, {{first_name}}, how are you ensuring {{company}} reps are connecting with ideal customer accounts, rather than just random booth traffic? Companies like Veeam and Palo Alto Networks use us to book 80% more pre-scheduled meetings with named accounts before events like this. Worth a peek?

Looks like you're busy at RSA, {{first_name}}. How are you making sure booth scans for {{company}} turn into qualified meetings within 48 hours, like Cisco and Palo Alto Networks who convert 70% compared to 15% before? Worth a look?

touches.subject: rsa event follow-up
touches.body: noticed {{first_name}}, {{company}} had reps at RSA Conference last week, which often means dealing with a flood of booth scans and follow-ups. how are you ensuring those initial conversations convert into pipeline before the leads go cold? companies like Cisco and Palo Alto Networks use us to convert 25% of their event leads to qualified meetings, compared to 5% previously, by automating the lead-to-CRM sync and personalized follow-ups. worth a look?

Noticed many at RSA were discussing the "72-hour half-life" of event leads, and how hard it is to get those booth scans to convert at {{company}}. How are you, {{first_name}}, ensuring those valuable RSA leads translate into qualified meetings for your reps before they go cold? Palo Alto Networks and CrowdStrike use us to convert 70% of event scans into first meetings within 72 hours, compared to 20% before, by automating the lead-to-CRM sync. Worth a look?
